title: Cod with Tomato & Pepper Couscous
summary: Zesty, steamy and seriously satisfying. This one-pot couscous dish makes the most of juicy peppers, flaky cod and punchy oregano. Fresh and fast.

method.description: Zest half the orange and set the zest aside. Cut two wedges from the orange for serving, then juice the rest into a measuring jug and top up with just-boiled water.

Heat the oil in a large ceramic casserole dish or lidded frying pan over a medium heat. Add the frozen peppers and cherry tomatoes, then pick over the oregano leaves. Cook for about 10 mins, stirring often, until the peppers have softened and the tomatoes are starting to burst.
Season the cod with salt and pepper. Nestle it into the veg and grate over the reserved orange zest. Cover with a lid and steam gently for 10-12 mins until the cod is opaque and flakes easily.

Meanwhile, tip the couscous into a bowl. Pour the orange juice and hot water mixture over the couscous. Cover with a plate and leave to stand for about 5 mins until all the liquid is absorbed. Fluff it up with a fork and season with salt and pepper.
Lift the cod out of the pan and set aside. Toss the couscous through the peppers and tomatoes until well combined.
Spoon the couscous onto plates and top with the cod. Pick over the parsley leaves and serve with the orange wedges on the side.

chefs.name: Anna Helm Baxter
chefs.summary: Anna Helm Baxter is a cookbook author, food stylist, and food producer at Mob. Having spent most of her life in the US, she loves potlucks, tailgates, and a good Thanksgiving spread. As a working mum of three, she gets the dinner struggle, which is why she’s all about recipes that are joyful, healthy, and totally doable. And if all else fails she always has cookies stashed in the freezer.
